title: LATAM Second Opinion Access Gap
problem: Patients in LATAM lack access to second opinions for serious diagnoses. Trust in local physicians is low in some regions. No easy way to consult with top specialists at leading hospitals. Patients travel internationally (to US, Spain) for second opinions, spending thousands. Misdiagnosis rates are unknown but suspected to be high.
solution: Second opinion platform connecting patients with specialists at leading LATAM hospitals (Hospital Israelita in São Paulo, Instituto Nacional de Ciencias Médicas in Mexico). Secure image/document sharing. Video consultation. Written second opinion report. Integration with local treating physicians.
technology: Secure document sharing, video consultation platform, matching algorithms, quality assurance workflows, mobile app
target_customer: Patients (B2C), health insurers (value-add benefit), self-insured employers
traction_signals: Medical tourism from LATAM to US growing
Proven models (Grand Rounds, Doctor on Demand)
regulatory_notes: Telemedicine regulations, cross-border practice of medicine, medical liability
varden_fit: MEDIUM FIT - Consumer-facing but infrastructure component (matching platform). Could start B2B with insurers. High-margin service.
notes: Second opinions are high-value, low-volume service. Target patients with serious diagnoses (cancer, cardiac surgery). Premium pricing justified by life-changing decisions.
